title: Phase 1: Memory Curation & Context Preservation
description: Daily Reflection (#1), Daily Log Indexing (#1b), and Pre-Compaction Context Flush (#6). These features transform the memory system from write-only to actively curated, giving the assistant searchable access to daily logs and automatically extracting key context before it's lost to compaction.

title: Phase 2: Heartbeat Improvements
description: Heartbeat State Diffing (#2) and Automatic Git Pull/Push (#2b). Reduces heartbeat noise by tracking what was already reported and automates git sync around heartbeat sessions so the agent always works on latest workspace state.

title: Phase 3: Integration API
description: Secure proxy service (integ-api) for external services (Gmail, Calendar). Provides hard process-level credential isolation, content filtering, rate limiting, and audit logging. Required by Phase 4 features.

title: Phase 4: Proactive Features
description: Draft Management System (#4) and Habits Tracking (#5). Highest-value proactive features that depend on integ-api for full power. Drafts auto-generate replies, habits track daily pillars with auto-detection.

title: Config schema extensions for new features
description:
Add Zod config schemas and defaults for all new features to the existing config system. This is a foundational task — all other tasks depend on these config fields existing.

Implementation steps:
1. Edit src/core/types.ts — add new schema sections:
   - ReflectionConfigSchema with fields: enabled (boolean, default true), schedule (string, default "0 7 * * *"), maxDailyLogEntries (number, default 500)
   - Extend MemoryConfigSchema with: indexDailyLogs (boolean, default true), dailyLogRetentionDays (number, default 90)
   - Extend SessionConfigSchema with: preCompactionFlush (boolean, default true)
   - Extend HeartbeatConfigSchema with: stateDiffing (boolean, default true), gitSync object containing enabled (boolean, default true) and remote (string, default "origin")
   - IntegApiConfigSchema with fields: enabled (boolean, default false), port (number, default 19100), bind (string, default "127.0.0.1"), inboundRateLimit (number, default 100), contentFilter object with redactPatterns (string array) and maxBodyLength (number, default 50000), services object with gmail and calendar sub-objects each having enabled (boolean) and scopes (string array)
   - HabitsConfigSchema with fields: enabled (boolean, default false), pillars (array of pillar objects)
   - DraftsConfigSchema with fields: enabled (boolean, default false), ttlHours (number, default 24), autoScan (boolean, default false)
2. Add all new schemas to the root ConfigSchema in src/core/types.ts with sensible defaults
3. Update settings.json defaults in src/core/config.ts DEFAULTS object to include new sections
4. Ensure backward compatibility — existing settings.json files without new fields should parse fine using Zod defaults

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given an existing settings.json without new fields, When loadConfig() is called, Then it parses successfully with all new fields set to defaults
2. GWT: Given a settings.json with reflection.enabled set to false, When loadConfig() is called, Then config.reflection.enabled is false
3. GWT: Given a settings.json with integApi.port set to 19200, When loadConfig() is called, Then config.integApi.port is 19200
4. Run existing tests with npx vitest run src/core/config.test.ts — must pass
5. Run full test suite with npx vitest run — no regressions

title: Daily log indexing into memory search
description:
Index daily JSONL audit logs into the vector store so they are searchable via hybrid memory search. Currently collectMemoryFiles() only indexes MEMORY.md and memory/*.md files. Daily logs contain the richest record of conversations and decisions but are invisible to search.

Implementation steps:
1. Edit src/memory/collect-files.ts:
   - Add new function collectDailyLogFiles(workspaceDir: string, retentionDays: number): string[] that scans {workspaceDir}/daily/ for .jsonl files, parses date from filename, returns only files within the retention window
   - Modify collectMemoryFiles() to accept optional config param { indexDailyLogs: boolean, dailyLogRetentionDays: number } and include daily log files when enabled
2. Edit src/memory/indexer.ts:
   - Add JSONL-aware text extraction: for files ending in .jsonl, parse each line as JSON, extract meaningful text (role + content fields from AuditEntry), skip tool_use/tool_result noise, format as readable text with timestamps
   - Add a preprocessContent(filePath: string, rawContent: string): string function that returns raw content for .md files and extracted text for .jsonl files
   - Call preprocessContent before chunking in syncFiles()
3. Update daemon.ts to pass the new config fields to collectMemoryFiles()
4. Update cli.ts mcp-server startup to also pass the config fields

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given a workspace with daily/2026-04-01.jsonl and retentionDays=90, When collectDailyLogFiles is called on 2026-04-03, Then the file is included
2. GWT: Given a daily log file older than retentionDays, When collectDailyLogFiles is called, Then it is excluded
3. GWT: Given a .jsonl file with interaction entries, When preprocessContent is called, Then it returns readable text with role and content, no raw tool JSON
4. GWT: Given daily log indexing is disabled in config, When collectMemoryFiles is called, Then no .jsonl files are returned
5. Run npx vitest run src/memory/collect-files.test.ts and src/memory/indexer.test.ts

title: Daily reflection — automated memory curation
description:
Create a daily reflection pipeline that reviews yesterday's daily JSONL audit log, extracts key decisions/lessons/facts via LLM, and writes them to memory/reflection-YYYY-MM-DD.md files for automatic indexing.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/memory/daily-reflection.ts with:
   - runDailyReflection(config: Config, workspaceDir: string): Promise<void> — orchestrates the pipeline
   - Reads yesterday's daily/{date}.jsonl using readAuditEntries() from src/memory/daily-log.ts
   - Filters to interaction entries (skip tool_call noise), formats as readable conversation text
   - Calls Anthropic API (using same pattern as session/compactor.ts summarizeWithAnthropic) with the REFLECTION_PROMPT template
   - Parses LLM response into structured items (decision, lesson, fact, project-update)
   - Writes output to {workspaceDir}/memory/reflection-{date}.md with YAML frontmatter (date, entry_count, categories)
   - Skips if reflection file already exists for that date (idempotent)
   - Skips if yesterday's log is empty or missing
2. Create src/templates/REFLECTION_PROMPT.md — the curation system prompt instructing the LLM to extract key decisions, lessons learned, important facts, and active project updates from daily log entries. Ignore routine tool calls and trivial exchanges.
3. Register as built-in cron-like job in daemon.ts startup sequence:
   - Use node-cron to schedule based on config.reflection.schedule (default "0 7 * * *")
   - Fire before the morning heartbeat
   - Run runDailyReflection() — errors are logged but don't block the daemon
4. The memory watcher + periodic resync in daemon.ts will automatically pick up new reflection files for indexing

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given yesterday's daily log with 10 interaction entries, When runDailyReflection runs, Then a memory/reflection-{yesterday}.md file is created with extracted items
2. GWT: Given reflection file already exists for yesterday, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it skips without overwriting
3. GWT: Given yesterday has no daily log, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it returns without error
4. GWT: Given a daily log with only tool_call entries and no interactions, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it skips (no meaningful content to extract)
5. GWT: Given config.reflection.enabled is false, When daemon starts, Then reflection cron job is not scheduled
6. Unit test the LLM call with a mock — verify prompt construction and response parsing
7. Verify the reflection file has valid YAML frontmatter and markdown body

title: Pre-compaction context flush
description:
Before the session compactor summarizes and truncates conversation history, flush key context (decisions, action items, important facts) to the daily log. This ensures nothing important disappears when messages are compacted away.

Implementation steps:
1. Edit src/session/compactor.ts:
   - Add new exported function flushPreCompactionContext(messages: ConversationTurn[], workspaceDir: string): Promise<void>
   - Takes the messages that are about to be compacted (the ones being summarized)
   - Calls Anthropic API (same pattern as summarizeConversation) with a shorter extraction prompt: "Extract key decisions, action items, and important facts from these messages. Return as a bulleted list."
   - Uses appendAuditEntry() from src/memory/daily-log.ts to write extracted items to today's daily log with type "interaction" and source "pre-compaction" in the context field
   - If the API call fails, log warning and continue — don't block compaction
2. Edit src/core/agent-runner.ts:
   - In the compaction path (where summarizeConversation is called), call flushPreCompactionContext() BEFORE summarizeConversation()
   - Only call if config.session.preCompactionFlush is true
   - Pass the conversation history that will be compacted
3. The pre-compaction context will be automatically picked up by daily log indexing (task #1b) and daily reflection (task #1)

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given a session with 30 messages about to be compacted, When flushPreCompactionContext is called, Then an audit entry with context "pre-compaction" is appended to today's daily log
2. GWT: Given the Anthropic API returns an error during flush, When flushPreCompactionContext is called, Then it logs a warning and returns without throwing
3. GWT: Given config.session.preCompactionFlush is false, When compaction triggers, Then flushPreCompactionContext is NOT called
4. GWT: Given a session with trivial messages only, When flushPreCompactionContext is called, Then it still writes the extraction (LLM decides what's important)
5. Unit test with mocked API — verify prompt construction includes the messages and daily log entry is written
6. Run npx vitest run src/session/compactor.test.ts — no regressions

title: Heartbeat state diffing
description:
Track what the heartbeat reported last time and only notify on actual changes — new events, changed statuses, resolved items. Eliminates repeated notifications about the same things.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/heartbeat/state.ts:
   - Define HeartbeatState type: { lastRun: string (ISO), snapshot: Record<string, string[]>, notifiedItems: string[] }
   - loadState(dataDir: string): HeartbeatState | null — reads {dataDir}/heartbeat-state.json, returns null if missing
   - saveState(dataDir: string, state: HeartbeatState): void — writes to {dataDir}/heartbeat-state.json with 0o600 permissions
   - diffState(previous: HeartbeatState | null, currentItems: string[]): { newItems: string[], resolvedItems: string[], unchanged: string[] } — compares previous notifiedItems with current items, returns categorized diff
2. Edit src/heartbeat/prompts.ts:
   - Import loadState, saveState, diffState from state.ts
   - Add new exported function buildDiffAwarePrompt(basePrompt: string, dataDir: string, currentContext: string[]): Promise<string>
   - If previous state exists, compute diff and append to prompt: "Changes since last heartbeat at {time}: New: [...]. Resolved: [...]. Unchanged count: N."
   - Save current state after building prompt
   - If stateDiffing is disabled or no previous state, return basePrompt unchanged
3. Edit daemon.ts heartbeat callback:
   - After resolveHeartbeatPrompt, call buildDiffAwarePrompt if config.heartbeat.stateDiffing is true
   - Pass config.security.dataDir for state file location

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given no previous heartbeat state, When buildDiffAwarePrompt is called, Then basePrompt is returned unchanged and initial state is saved
2. GWT: Given previous state with items ["meeting at 3pm"], When current items are ["meeting at 3pm", "new email"], Then diff shows "new email" as new and "meeting at 3pm" as unchanged
3. GWT: Given previous state with items ["task A"], When current items are [], Then diff shows "task A" as resolved
4. GWT: Given config.heartbeat.stateDiffing is false, When heartbeat fires, Then no state diffing occurs
5. Unit test loadState/saveState with temp directory
6. Unit test diffState with various scenarios (empty, same, added, removed, mixed)
7. Run npx vitest run src/heartbeat/ — no regressions

title: Automatic git pull/push around heartbeat
description:
Automate git pull (with stash) before each heartbeat session and git push after it completes. The agent still makes commits — this only automates transport so the workspace is always current.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/heartbeat/git-sync.ts:
   - pullWorkspace(workspaceDir: string, remote: string): Promise<{ success: boolean, stashed: boolean, error?: string }>
     - Check if working tree is dirty with execFile("git", ["status", "--porcelain"])
     - If dirty, run execFile("git", ["stash", "push", "-m", "heartbeat-auto-stash"])
     - Run execFile("git", ["pull", "--rebase", remote])
     - If stashed, run execFile("git", ["stash", "pop"])
     - If any step fails, log warning and return { success: false, error }
     - Use child_process.execFile (not shell) for safety
   - pushWorkspace(workspaceDir: string, remote: string): Promise<{ success: boolean, error?: string }>
     - Check if there are local commits ahead of remote with execFile("git", ["rev-list", "--count", "@{u}..HEAD"])
     - If count > 0, run execFile("git", ["push", remote])
     - Return success/error
   - Both functions accept workspaceDir as cwd option and use pino logger
2. Edit daemon.ts heartbeat callback:
   - Before enqueuing heartbeat message, call pullWorkspace() if config.heartbeat.gitSync.enabled
   - After heartbeat turn completes (need to hook into queue processing result), call pushWorkspace()
   - For push-after-heartbeat: modify the heartbeat message to include metadata flag, and in processLoop check for it after turn completion
   - Alternative simpler approach: schedule pushWorkspace via setTimeout(pushWorkspace, 60000) after the heartbeat fires to give the agent time to work

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given a clean workspace, When pullWorkspace is called, Then git pull --rebase runs and returns success
2. GWT: Given a dirty workspace, When pullWorkspace is called, Then it stashes, pulls, and pops stash
3. GWT: Given a pull conflict, When pullWorkspace is called, Then it returns { success: false } with error message and doesn't crash
4. GWT: Given local commits ahead of remote, When pushWorkspace is called, Then git push runs
5. GWT: Given no local commits, When pushWorkspace is called, Then it skips push and returns success
6. GWT: Given config.heartbeat.gitSync.enabled is false, When heartbeat fires, Then no git operations occur
7. Unit test with mocked execFile
8. Run npx vitest run src/heartbeat/ — no regressions

title: Integ-API core server and router
description:
Build the core integ-api HTTP server infrastructure: lightweight router, middleware pipeline, and server lifecycle. Uses Node.js built-in http module (zero new deps). This is the foundation for all integration modules.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/server.ts:
   - SimpleRouter class (~50-80 lines): method+path matching with :param extraction, JSON request/response helpers
   - Methods: router.get(path, handler), router.post(path, handler), router.use(middleware)
   - Handler signature: (req: ParsedRequest, res: JsonResponse) => Promise<void>
   - ParsedRequest extends IncomingMessage with: params (Record<string, string>), query (URLSearchParams), body (parsed JSON)
   - JsonResponse wraps ServerResponse with: json(data, status?), error(IntegApiError)
   - createIntegApiServer(config: IntegApiConfig): { start(): Promise<void>, stop(): Promise<void>, router: SimpleRouter }
   - Binds to config.integApi.bind (default 127.0.0.1) on config.integApi.port (default 19100)
   - Localhost-only binding enforced — reject non-loopback bind addresses
2. Create src/integ-api/types.ts:
   - IntegApiError interface: { error: "rate_limited" | "auth_failed" | "service_unavailable" | "not_found", message: string, retryAfterMs?: number, service: string, profilesTried?: number }
   - IntegrationManifest interface: { id: string, name: string, status: string, capabilities: string[], endpoints: EndpointDef[], rateLimits: { requestsPerMinute: number } }
   - IntegrationModule interface: { id: string, manifest: IntegrationManifest, routes: (router: SimpleRouter) => void, healthCheck: () => Promise<boolean> }
3. Create src/integ-api/middleware/audit.ts:
   - JSONL request/response audit logger
   - Logs to {dataDir}/integ-api/audit.jsonl with timestamp, method, path, status, duration
4. Add GET /integ-api/health endpoint that returns { status: "ok", uptime: N }

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given server is started, When GET /integ-api/health is called, Then it returns 200 with { status: "ok" }
2. GWT: Given a route GET /test/:id is registered, When GET /test/123 is called, Then handler receives params.id = "123"
3. GWT: Given server binds to 127.0.0.1, When an external interface is specified, Then it rejects with error
4. GWT: Given a request is made, When audit middleware is active, Then a JSONL line is written to audit log
5. GWT: Given server.stop() is called, When pending connections exist, Then server closes gracefully
6. Unit test SimpleRouter path matching with various patterns
7. Unit test JSON body parsing (valid JSON, invalid JSON, empty body)
8. Run npx vitest run src/integ-api/

title: Auth manager with credential storage and rotation
description:
Implement the auth manager with secure credential storage, token refresh, profile rotation, and cooldown logic. Handles OAuth2 tokens for all integrations.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/auth/store.ts:
   - Credential storage at {dataDir}/integ-api/credentials/ with 0o600 file permissions
   - saveCredentials(profileId: string, credentials: object): Promise<void>
   - loadCredentials(profileId: string): Promise<object | null>
   - deleteCredentials(profileId: string): Promise<void>
   - Credentials stored as JSON files: {profileId}.json
   - Ensure directory exists with 0o700 on first write
2. Create src/integ-api/auth/manager.ts:
   - AuthProfile type: { id: string, service: string, type: "oauth2", credentials: { clientId, clientSecret, refreshToken, accessToken, expiresAt } }
   - createAuthManager(store: CredentialStore): AuthManager
   - AuthManager interface:
     - registerProfile(profile: AuthProfileConfig): void
     - getAccessToken(serviceId: string): Promise<{ token: string, profileId: string }> — tries profiles in order, refreshes if expired
     - refreshToken(profileId: string): Promise<string> — calls OAuth2 token endpoint with refresh_token grant
     - markFailed(profileId: string): void — increments failure count, activates cooldown after N failures
   - Rotation logic: on 401/403, try next profile; after all fail, return auth_failed error
   - Cooldown: after 3 consecutive failures, back off 5 minutes before retrying a profile
   - Token refresh: POST to Google OAuth2 token endpoint with refresh_token grant type
3. Create src/integ-api/auth/oauth-setup.ts:
   - Interactive OAuth2 setup flow for Google services
   - Generates authorization URL, opens browser, listens for callback on localhost
   - Exchanges code for tokens, saves via store.ts
   - Used by: pa integapi auth google

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given valid OAuth2 credentials, When getAccessToken is called, Then it returns the current access token
2. GWT: Given an expired access token, When getAccessToken is called, Then it refreshes and returns new token
3. GWT: Given refresh fails for profile[0], When getAccessToken is called, Then it tries profile[1]
4. GWT: Given all profiles fail, When getAccessToken is called, Then it returns auth_failed error with profilesTried count
5. GWT: Given a profile has 3 consecutive failures, When getAccessToken is called within cooldown, Then it skips that profile
6. GWT: Given saveCredentials is called, When the file is written, Then permissions are 0o600
7. Unit test rotation logic with 2 mock profiles
8. Unit test cooldown timer resets after success

title: Rate limiting and content filter middleware
description:
Implement inbound rate limiting (protects integ-api) and content filtering pipeline (redact, anonymize, truncate) as middleware for the integ-api server.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/middleware/inbound-rate-limiter.ts:
   - Sliding window rate limiter per caller (for integ-api, caller is always the PA process, but designed for future multi-caller)
   - createInboundRateLimiter(maxPerMinute: number): Middleware
   - Uses in-memory token bucket or sliding window counter
   - On limit exceeded: return 429 with IntegApiError { error: "rate_limited", retryAfterMs: N }
   - Reset counters periodically to prevent memory leak
2. Create src/integ-api/middleware/content-filter.ts:
   - createContentFilter(config: { redactPatterns: string[], maxBodyLength: number }): ContentFilter
   - ContentFilter.filter(data: unknown): unknown — recursively traverses JSON response
   - Redaction: applies regex patterns from config to all string values, replaces matches with [REDACTED]
   - Default patterns: email-like strings in sensitive fields, potential API keys (long alphanumeric strings), credit card numbers
   - Truncation: if serialized response exceeds maxBodyLength, truncate with "[...truncated]" marker
   - Structured output: ensure the filtered response is always valid JSON
3. Wire both middleware into the integ-api server pipeline in server.ts:
   - Request flow: inbound rate limiter -> route handler -> content filter -> audit -> response

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given rate limit of 5/min, When 6 requests are made in 1 minute, Then 6th returns 429 with retryAfterMs
2. GWT: Given rate limit window passes, When a new request arrives, Then it succeeds
3. GWT: Given a response containing "sk-abc123def456", When content filter runs with default patterns, Then it's replaced with [REDACTED]
4. GWT: Given a response body of 100KB, When maxBodyLength is 50000, Then response is truncated with marker
5. GWT: Given a deeply nested JSON response with sensitive strings, When filter runs, Then all matching strings at any depth are redacted
6. GWT: Given redactPatterns is empty array, When filter runs, Then response passes through unchanged
7. Unit test rate limiter with time-mocked requests
8. Unit test content filter with various data shapes (string, array, nested object, null)

title: Gmail integration module
description:
Implement the integration module registry with discovery endpoint and the Gmail read-only integration module. Gmail provides list, read, search, and labels endpoints via Google Gmail API.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/integrations/registry.ts:
   - createRegistry(): IntegrationRegistry
   - register(module: IntegrationModule): void
   - getModule(id: string): IntegrationModule | undefined
   - getAllManifests(): IntegrationManifest[]
   - Register GET /integ-api/integrations route that returns { integrations: manifest[] }
2. Create src/integ-api/integrations/types.ts (if not already in integ-api/types.ts):
   - Re-export or define IntegrationModule, IntegrationManifest, EndpointDef
3. Create src/integ-api/integrations/gmail/index.ts implementing IntegrationModule:
   - id: "gmail", manifest with capabilities: ["list", "read", "search", "labels"]
   - Endpoints: GET /gmail/messages, GET /gmail/messages/:id, GET /gmail/labels, GET /gmail/search
4. Create src/integ-api/integrations/gmail/routes.ts:
   - GET /gmail/messages?query=X&max=N&labelIds=Y — calls Gmail API /users/me/messages with query params, returns list with snippet preview
   - GET /gmail/messages/:id — calls Gmail API /users/me/messages/:id?format=full, parses MIME parts to extract plain text body, subject, from, to, date
   - GET /gmail/labels — calls Gmail API /users/me/labels, returns label list
   - GET /gmail/search?q=X — alias for messages with query param
   - All routes use AuthManager.getAccessToken("gmail") for Bearer token
   - All responses go through content filter
   - Outbound rate limiting: 60 requests/minute for Gmail API
5. Create src/integ-api/integrations/gmail/rate-limits.ts:
   - Gmail API quota config: 60 req/min per user

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given gmail module is registered, When GET /integ-api/integrations is called, Then gmail appears in response with correct capabilities
2. GWT: Given valid Gmail auth, When GET /gmail/messages?max=5 is called, Then it returns up to 5 message summaries
3. GWT: Given a specific message ID, When GET /gmail/messages/:id is called, Then it returns parsed message with subject, from, body text
4. GWT: Given Gmail API returns 401, When a route is called, Then auth manager refreshes token and retries
5. GWT: Given Gmail API rate limit hit, When requests exceed 60/min, Then outbound limiter queues or returns structured error
6. Unit test Gmail API response parsing (MIME to plain text extraction)
7. Unit test route registration and path matching
8. Integration test with mocked Gmail API responses

title: Calendar integration module
description:
Implement the Google Calendar read-only integration module. Provides today's events, week view, event details, and free/busy queries via Google Calendar API.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/integrations/calendar/index.ts implementing IntegrationModule:
   - id: "calendar", manifest with capabilities: ["today", "week", "event", "free-busy"]
   - Endpoints: GET /calendar/today, GET /calendar/week, GET /calendar/event/:id, GET /calendar/free-busy
2. Create src/integ-api/integrations/calendar/routes.ts:
   - GET /calendar/today — calls Calendar API /calendars/primary/events with timeMin=today 00:00, timeMax=today 23:59, returns sorted event list with title, time, location, description
   - GET /calendar/week — same but timeMin=today, timeMax=today+7 days
   - GET /calendar/event/:id — calls Calendar API /calendars/primary/events/:id, returns full event details
   - GET /calendar/free-busy — calls Calendar API /freeBusy with timeMin/timeMax from query params, returns busy intervals
   - All routes use AuthManager.getAccessToken("calendar") for Bearer token
   - All responses through content filter
3. Create src/integ-api/integrations/calendar/rate-limits.ts:
   - Calendar API quota config: 60 req/min
4. Register calendar module in registry alongside gmail

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given calendar module is registered, When GET /integ-api/integrations is called, Then calendar appears with correct capabilities
2. GWT: Given valid Calendar auth, When GET /calendar/today is called, Then it returns today's events sorted by start time
3. GWT: Given a specific event ID, When GET /calendar/event/:id is called, Then full event details are returned
4. GWT: Given no events today, When GET /calendar/today is called, Then it returns empty array (not error)
5. GWT: Given Calendar API returns 401, When a route is called, Then auth manager handles token refresh
6. Unit test event date/time parsing and timezone handling
7. Integration test with mocked Calendar API responses

title: CLI facade, daemon integration, and MCP tools
description:
Create the pa integapi CLI facade and integrate the integ-api server lifecycle with the daemon. The CLI wraps HTTP calls to the integ-api server so the assistant's MCP tools can use it.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/cli.ts:
   - Subcommands: serve, list, health, gmail (list/read/labels), calendar (today/week/event), auth (google)
   - Each subcommand makes HTTP request to http://127.0.0.1:{port}/... and formats response
   - serve: starts the integ-api server as a standalone process
   - list: GET /integ-api/integrations, formats as table
   - health: GET /integ-api/health for each integration, shows status
   - gmail list [--query X] [--max N]: GET /gmail/messages
   - gmail read <id>: GET /gmail/messages/:id
   - gmail labels: GET /gmail/labels
   - calendar today: GET /calendar/today
   - calendar week: GET /calendar/week
   - calendar event <id>: GET /calendar/event/:id
   - auth google: delegates to oauth-setup.ts interactive flow
2. Edit src/cli.ts:
   - Add "integapi" to VALID_COMMANDS
   - Route to integ-api CLI handler
3. Edit src/daemon.ts:
   - If config.integApi.enabled, spawn integ-api as a separate child process using child_process.fork() or spawn()
   - Pass config via environment or temp config file
   - Monitor child process health, restart on crash (with backoff)
   - On daemon shutdown, terminate integ-api child process
4. Create src/tools/integ-server.ts — new MCP server:
   - Tools that wrap pa integapi CLI calls for the agent
   - integ_gmail tool: { action: "list"|"read"|"labels"|"search", params }
   - integ_calendar tool: { action: "today"|"week"|"event"|"free-busy", params }
   - integ_discover tool: returns available integrations
5. Register integ-server as MCP server in daemon.ts alongside memory and assistant servers

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given integ-api is running, When pa integapi list is called, Then available integrations are printed
2. GWT: Given integ-api is running, When pa integapi health is called, Then health status for each integration is shown
3. GWT: Given daemon starts with integApi.enabled true, When checking processes, Then integ-api child process is running
4. GWT: Given integ-api child crashes, When daemon health check runs, Then child is restarted
5. GWT: Given daemon shuts down, When SIGTERM is sent, Then integ-api child is also terminated
6. GWT: Given MCP integ_gmail tool is called with action "list", When agent invokes it, Then it returns formatted email list
7. Test CLI subcommand parsing for all commands

title: Habits tracking system
description:
Implement the habits tracking system. A HABITS.md file defines daily pillars (areas of improvement). The heartbeat tracks progress, auto-detects objective achievements, and nudges for unchecked items.

Implementation steps:

title: Draft management system
description:
Implement the draft management system. The heartbeat detects messages needing replies (via integ-api Gmail/Slack), generates drafts in the user's voice, and manages their lifecycle (active -> sent/expired).

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/drafts/manager.ts:
   - Draft type: { id: string, type: "email"|"slack", sourceId: string, recipient: string, subject: string, status: "active"|"sent"|"expired", createdAt: string, originalMessage: string, draftReply: string }
   - createDraft(workspace: string, draft: Draft): Promise<string> — writes to workspace/drafts/active/{id}.md with YAML frontmatter + original message + draft reply
   - listDrafts(workspace: string, status?: string): Promise<Draft[]> — reads from workspace/drafts/{status}/ directories
   - readDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<Draft | null>
   - approveDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<void> — moves from active/ to sent/
   - discardDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<void> — deletes the draft file
   - Ensure workspace/drafts/{active,sent,expired}/ directories exist
2. Create src/drafts/scanner.ts:
   - scanForReplyNeeded(config: Config): Promise<Array<{ type: string, sourceId: string, recipient: string, subject: string, body: string }>>
   - For Gmail: calls pa integapi gmail list --query "is:unread" and filters messages that look like they need replies (from known contacts, direct questions, etc.)
   - For Slack: checks recent DMs via Slack adapter (if available)
   - Returns list of items needing replies
3. Create src/drafts/generator.ts:
   - generateDraft(originalMessage: string, userContext: string, searchMemory: Function): Promise<string>

   - draft_list: list active/sent/expired drafts
   - draft_read: read a specific draft

title: Daily reflection — automated memory curation
description:
Create a daily reflection pipeline that reviews yesterday's daily JSONL audit log, extracts key decisions/lessons/facts via LLM, and writes them to memory/reflection-YYYY-MM-DD.md files for automatic indexing.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/memory/daily-reflection.ts with:
   - runDailyReflection(config: Config, workspaceDir: string): Promise<void> — orchestrates the pipeline
   - Reads yesterday's daily/{date}.jsonl using readAuditEntries() from src/memory/daily-log.ts
   - Filters to interaction entries (skip tool_call noise), formats as readable conversation text
   - Calls Anthropic API (using same pattern as session/compactor.ts summarizeWithAnthropic) with the REFLECTION_PROMPT template
   - Parses LLM response into structured items (decision, lesson, fact, project-update)
   - Writes output to {workspaceDir}/memory/reflection-{date}.md with YAML frontmatter (date, entry_count, categories)
   - Skips if reflection file already exists for that date (idempotent)
   - Skips if yesterday's log is empty or missing
2. Create src/templates/REFLECTION_PROMPT.md — the curation system prompt instructing the LLM to extract key decisions, lessons learned, important facts, and active project updates from daily log entries. Ignore routine tool calls and trivial exchanges.
3. Register as built-in cron-like job in daemon.ts startup sequence:
   - Use node-cron to schedule based on config.reflection.schedule (default "0 7 * * *")
   - Fire before the morning heartbeat
   - Run runDailyReflection() — errors are logged but don't block the daemon
4. The memory watcher + periodic resync in daemon.ts will automatically pick up new reflection files for indexing

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given yesterday's daily log with 10 interaction entries, When runDailyReflection runs, Then a memory/reflection-{yesterday}.md file is created with extracted items
2. GWT: Given reflection file already exists for yesterday, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it skips without overwriting
3. GWT: Given yesterday has no daily log, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it returns without error
4. GWT: Given a daily log with only tool_call entries and no interactions, When runDailyReflection runs, Then it skips (no meaningful content to extract)
5. GWT: Given config.reflection.enabled is false, When daemon starts, Then reflection cron job is not scheduled
6. Unit test the LLM call with a mock — verify prompt construction and response parsing
7. Verify the reflection file has valid YAML frontmatter and markdown body

title: Pre-compaction context flush
description:
Before the session compactor summarizes and truncates conversation history, flush key context (decisions, action items, important facts) to the daily log. This ensures nothing important disappears when messages are compacted away.

Implementation steps:
1. Edit src/session/compactor.ts:
   - Add new exported function flushPreCompactionContext(messages: ConversationTurn[], workspaceDir: string): Promise<void>
   - Takes the messages that are about to be compacted (the ones being summarized)
   - Calls Anthropic API (same pattern as summarizeConversation) with a shorter extraction prompt: "Extract key decisions, action items, and important facts from these messages. Return as a bulleted list."
   - Uses appendAuditEntry() from src/memory/daily-log.ts to write extracted items to today's daily log with type "interaction" and source "pre-compaction" in the context field
   - If the API call fails, log warning and continue — don't block compaction
2. Edit src/core/agent-runner.ts:
   - In the compaction path (where summarizeConversation is called), call flushPreCompactionContext() BEFORE summarizeConversation()
   - Only call if config.session.preCompactionFlush is true
   - Pass the conversation history that will be compacted
3. The pre-compaction context will be automatically picked up by daily log indexing (task #1b) and daily reflection (task #1)

Verification/Test steps:

2. GWT: Given the Anthropic API returns an error during flush, When flushPreCompactionContext is called, Then it logs a warning and returns without throwing
3. GWT: Given config.session.preCompactionFlush is false, When compaction triggers, Then flushPreCompactionContext is NOT called
4. GWT: Given a session with trivial messages only, When flushPreCompactionContext is called, Then it still writes the extraction (LLM decides what's important)

title: Heartbeat state diffing
description:
Track what the heartbeat reported last time and only notify on actual changes — new events, changed statuses, resolved items. Eliminates repeated notifications about the same things.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/heartbeat/state.ts:
   - Define HeartbeatState type: { lastRun: string (ISO), snapshot: Record<string, string[]>, notifiedItems: string[] }
   - loadState(dataDir: string): HeartbeatState | null — reads {dataDir}/heartbeat-state.json, returns null if missing
   - saveState(dataDir: string, state: HeartbeatState): void — writes to {dataDir}/heartbeat-state.json with 0o600 permissions
   - diffState(previous: HeartbeatState | null, currentItems: string[]): { newItems: string[], resolvedItems: string[], unchanged: string[] } — compares previous notifiedItems with current items, returns categorized diff
2. Edit src/heartbeat/prompts.ts:
   - Import loadState, saveState, diffState from state.ts
   - Add new exported function buildDiffAwarePrompt(basePrompt: string, dataDir: string, currentContext: string[]): Promise<string>
   - If previous state exists, compute diff and append to prompt: "Changes since last heartbeat at {time}: New: [...]. Resolved: [...]. Unchanged count: N."
   - Save current state after building prompt
   - If stateDiffing is disabled or no previous state, return basePrompt unchanged
3. Edit daemon.ts heartbeat callback:
   - After resolveHeartbeatPrompt, call buildDiffAwarePrompt if config.heartbeat.stateDiffing is true

title: Automatic git pull/push around heartbeat
description:
Automate git pull (with stash) before each heartbeat session and git push after it completes. The agent still makes commits — this only automates transport so the workspace is always current.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/heartbeat/git-sync.ts:
   - pullWorkspace(workspaceDir: string, remote: string): Promise<{ success: boolean, stashed: boolean, error?: string }>
     - Check if working tree is dirty with execFile("git", ["status", "--porcelain"])
     - If dirty, run execFile("git", ["stash", "push", "-m", "heartbeat-auto-stash"])
     - Run execFile("git", ["pull", "--rebase", remote])
     - If stashed, run execFile("git", ["stash", "pop"])
     - If any step fails, log warning and return { success: false, error }
     - Use child_process.execFile (not shell) for safety
   - pushWorkspace(workspaceDir: string, remote: string): Promise<{ success: boolean, error?: string }>
     - Check if there are local commits ahead of remote with execFile("git", ["rev-list", "--count", "@{u}..HEAD"])
     - If count > 0, run execFile("git", ["push", remote])
     - Return success/error
   - Both functions accept workspaceDir as cwd option and use pino logger
2. Edit daemon.ts heartbeat callback:
   - Before enqueuing heartbeat message, call pullWorkspace() if config.heartbeat.gitSync.enabled
   - After heartbeat turn completes (need to hook into queue processing result), call pushWorkspace()
   - For push-after-heartbeat: modify the heartbeat message to include metadata flag, and in processLoop check for it after turn completion
   - Alternative simpler approach: schedule pushWorkspace via setTimeout(pushWorkspace, 60000) after the heartbeat fires to give the agent time to work

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given a clean workspace, When pullWorkspace is called, Then git pull --rebase runs and returns success
2. GWT: Given a dirty workspace, When pullWorkspace is called, Then it stashes, pulls, and pops stash
3. GWT: Given a pull conflict, When pullWorkspace is called, Then it returns { success: false } with error message and doesn't crash
4. GWT: Given local commits ahead of remote, When pushWorkspace is called, Then git push runs
5. GWT: Given no local commits, When pushWorkspace is called, Then it skips push and returns success
6. GWT: Given config.heartbeat.gitSync.enabled is false, When heartbeat fires, Then no git operations occur
7. Unit test with mocked execFile
8. Run npx vitest run src/heartbeat/ — no regressions

title: Integ-API core server and router
description:
Build the core integ-api HTTP server infrastructure: lightweight router, middleware pipeline, and server lifecycle. Uses Node.js built-in http module (zero new deps). This is the foundation for all integration modules.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/server.ts:
   - SimpleRouter class (~50-80 lines): method+path matching with :param extraction, JSON request/response helpers
   - Methods: router.get(path, handler), router.post(path, handler), router.use(middleware)
   - Handler signature: (req: ParsedRequest, res: JsonResponse) => Promise<void>
   - ParsedRequest extends IncomingMessage with: params (Record<string, string>), query (URLSearchParams), body (parsed JSON)
   - JsonResponse wraps ServerResponse with: json(data, status?), error(IntegApiError)
   - createIntegApiServer(config: IntegApiConfig): { start(): Promise<void>, stop(): Promise<void>, router: SimpleRouter }
   - Binds to config.integApi.bind (default 127.0.0.1) on config.integApi.port (default 19100)
   - Localhost-only binding enforced — reject non-loopback bind addresses
2. Create src/integ-api/types.ts:
   - IntegApiError interface: { error: "rate_limited" | "auth_failed" | "service_unavailable" | "not_found", message: string, retryAfterMs?: number, service: string, profilesTried?: number }
   - IntegrationManifest interface: { id: string, name: string, status: string, capabilities: string[], endpoints: EndpointDef[], rateLimits: { requestsPerMinute: number } }
   - IntegrationModule interface: { id: string, manifest: IntegrationManifest, routes: (router: SimpleRouter) => void, healthCheck: () => Promise<boolean> }
3. Create src/integ-api/middleware/audit.ts:
   - JSONL request/response audit logger
   - Logs to {dataDir}/integ-api/audit.jsonl with timestamp, method, path, status, duration
4. Add GET /integ-api/health endpoint that returns { status: "ok", uptime: N }

title: Auth manager with credential storage and rotation
description:
Implement the auth manager with secure credential storage, token refresh, profile rotation, and cooldown logic. Handles OAuth2 tokens for all integrations.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/auth/store.ts:
   - Credential storage at {dataDir}/integ-api/credentials/ with 0o600 file permissions
   - saveCredentials(profileId: string, credentials: object): Promise<void>

   - createAuthManager(store: CredentialStore): AuthManager
   - AuthManager interface:
     - registerProfile(profile: AuthProfileConfig): void

     - refreshToken(profileId: string): Promise<string> — calls OAuth2 token endpoint with refresh_token grant
     - markFailed(profileId: string): void — increments failure count, activates cooldown after N failures
   - Rotation logic: on 401/403, try next profile; after all fail, return auth_failed error
   - Cooldown: after 3 consecutive failures, back off 5 minutes before retrying a profile

title: Rate limiting and content filter middleware
description:
Implement inbound rate limiting (protects integ-api) and content filtering pipeline (redact, anonymize, truncate) as middleware for the integ-api server.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/middleware/inbound-rate-limiter.ts:
   - Sliding window rate limiter per caller (for integ-api, caller is always the PA process, but designed for future multi-caller)
   - createInboundRateLimiter(maxPerMinute: number): Middleware
   - Uses in-memory token bucket or sliding window counter
   - On limit exceeded: return 429 with IntegApiError { error: "rate_limited", retryAfterMs: N }
   - Reset counters periodically to prevent memory leak
2. Create src/integ-api/middleware/content-filter.ts:
   - createContentFilter(config: { redactPatterns: string[], maxBodyLength: number }): ContentFilter
   - ContentFilter.filter(data: unknown): unknown — recursively traverses JSON response
   - Redaction: applies regex patterns from config to all string values, replaces matches with [REDACTED]
   - Default patterns: email-like strings in sensitive fields, potential API keys (long alphanumeric strings), credit card numbers
   - Truncation: if serialized response exceeds maxBodyLength, truncate with "[...truncated]" marker
   - Structured output: ensure the filtered response is always valid JSON
3. Wire both middleware into the integ-api server pipeline in server.ts:
   - Request flow: inbound rate limiter -> route handler -> content filter -> audit -> response

title: Calendar integration module
description:
Implement the Google Calendar read-only integration module. Provides today's events, week view, event details, and free/busy queries via Google Calendar API.

title: CLI facade, daemon integration, and MCP tools
description:
Create the pa integapi CLI facade and integrate the integ-api server lifecycle with the daemon. The CLI wraps HTTP calls to the integ-api server so the assistant's MCP tools can use it.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/integ-api/cli.ts:
   - Subcommands: serve, list, health, gmail (list/read/labels), calendar (today/week/event), auth (google)
   - Each subcommand makes HTTP request to http://127.0.0.1:{port}/... and formats response
   - serve: starts the integ-api server as a standalone process
   - list: GET /integ-api/integrations, formats as table
   - health: GET /integ-api/health for each integration, shows status
   - gmail list [--query X] [--max N]: GET /gmail/messages
   - gmail read <id>: GET /gmail/messages/:id
   - gmail labels: GET /gmail/labels
   - calendar today: GET /calendar/today
   - calendar week: GET /calendar/week
   - calendar event <id>: GET /calendar/event/:id
   - auth google: delegates to oauth-setup.ts interactive flow
2. Edit src/cli.ts:
   - Add "integapi" to VALID_COMMANDS
   - Route to integ-api CLI handler
3. Edit src/daemon.ts:
   - If config.integApi.enabled, spawn integ-api as a separate child process using child_process.fork() or spawn()
   - Pass config via environment or temp config file
   - Monitor child process health, restart on crash (with backoff)
   - On daemon shutdown, terminate integ-api child process
4. Create src/tools/integ-server.ts — new MCP server:
   - Tools that wrap pa integapi CLI calls for the agent
   - integ_gmail tool: { action: "list"|"read"|"labels"|"search", params }
   - integ_calendar tool: { action: "today"|"week"|"event"|"free-busy", params }
   - integ_discover tool: returns available integrations
5. Register integ-server as MCP server in daemon.ts alongside memory and assistant servers

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given integ-api is running, When pa integapi list is called, Then available integrations are printed
2. GWT: Given integ-api is running, When pa integapi health is called, Then health status for each integration is shown
3. GWT: Given daemon starts with integApi.enabled true, When checking processes, Then integ-api child process is running
4. GWT: Given integ-api child crashes, When daemon health check runs, Then child is restarted
5. GWT: Given daemon shuts down, When SIGTERM is sent, Then integ-api child is also terminated
6. GWT: Given MCP integ_gmail tool is called with action "list", When agent invokes it, Then it returns formatted email list
7. Test CLI subcommand parsing for all commands
8. Test MCP tool registration and response formatting

title: Habits tracking system
description:
Implement the habits tracking system. A HABITS.md file defines daily pillars (areas of improvement). The heartbeat tracks progress, auto-detects objective achievements, and nudges for unchecked items.

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/templates/HABITS.md — default template with 3 sample pillars:
   - "Code Commit" (auto-detect: git log --since=today in workspace)
   - "Exercise" (self-reported)
   - "Reading" (self-reported)
   - Format: YAML frontmatter with pillar definitions (name, type: auto|manual, detection_command for auto, description), then a daily checklist section, then a History section
2. Create src/heartbeat/habits.ts:
   - Pillar type: { name: string, type: "auto" | "manual", detectionCommand?: string, description: string }
   - loadHabits(workspacePath: string): Promise<{ pillars: Pillar[], checklist: Record<string, boolean>, historySection: string }>
   - checkAutoHabits(workspacePath: string, pillars: Pillar[]): Promise<Record<string, boolean>> — runs detection commands from a safe whitelist (git, wc, ls, cat — no arbitrary commands), returns which auto-pillars are fulfilled
   - resetDaily(workspacePath: string): Promise<void> — archive yesterday's checklist to History section, create fresh unchecked checklist for today
   - markHabit(workspacePath: string, pillarName: string, done: boolean): Promise<void> — update checklist in HABITS.md
   - formatHabitStatus(checklist: Record<string, boolean>, pillars: Pillar[]): string — generates status string like "[x] Code Commit  [ ] Exercise  [x] Reading"
   - Safe command whitelist: only allow git, wc, ls, cat, grep, date, stat as detection commands
3. Integrate with heartbeat prompts in src/heartbeat/prompts.ts:
   - In morning heartbeat: call resetDaily() first, then include fresh checklist status
   - In regular heartbeat: call checkAutoHabits(), include updated status
   - In evening heartbeat: include final status with nudges for unchecked items
4. Add MCP tool in assistant-server.ts or new habits-server:
   - habit_check tool: { pillar: string, done: boolean } — marks a self-reported habit
   - habit_status tool: returns current checklist
5. Seed HABITS.md in workspace via ensureWorkspace() in src/core/workspace.ts

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given HABITS.md with 3 pillars, When loadHabits is called, Then all pillars and checklist state are returned
2. GWT: Given a "Code Commit" pillar with detection command "git log --since=today", When checkAutoHabits runs and git has today's commits, Then pillar is marked true
3. GWT: Given yesterday's checklist has entries, When resetDaily runs, Then yesterday is archived to History and fresh unchecked checklist is created
4. GWT: Given a detection command containing "rm" or "curl", When checkAutoHabits validates it, Then command is rejected (not in safe whitelist)
5. GWT: Given habit_check tool is called with pillar="Exercise" done=true, When HABITS.md is read, Then Exercise is checked
6. GWT: Given config.habits.enabled is false, When heartbeat fires, Then no habits processing occurs
7. Unit test formatHabitStatus output format
8. Unit test safe command whitelist validation
9. Run npx vitest run src/heartbeat/habits.test.ts

title: Draft management system
description:
Implement the draft management system. The heartbeat detects messages needing replies (via integ-api Gmail/Slack), generates drafts in the user's voice, and manages their lifecycle (active -> sent/expired).

Implementation steps:
1. Create src/drafts/manager.ts:
   - Draft type: { id: string, type: "email"|"slack", sourceId: string, recipient: string, subject: string, status: "active"|"sent"|"expired", createdAt: string, originalMessage: string, draftReply: string }
   - createDraft(workspace: string, draft: Draft): Promise<string> — writes to workspace/drafts/active/{id}.md with YAML frontmatter + original message + draft reply
   - listDrafts(workspace: string, status?: string): Promise<Draft[]> — reads from workspace/drafts/{status}/ directories
   - readDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<Draft | null>
   - approveDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<void> — moves from active/ to sent/
   - discardDraft(workspace: string, id: string): Promise<void> — deletes the draft file
   - Ensure workspace/drafts/{active,sent,expired}/ directories exist
2. Create src/drafts/scanner.ts:
   - scanForReplyNeeded(config: Config): Promise<Array<{ type: string, sourceId: string, recipient: string, subject: string, body: string }>>
   - For Gmail: calls pa integapi gmail list --query "is:unread" and filters messages that look like they need replies (from known contacts, direct questions, etc.)
   - For Slack: checks recent DMs via Slack adapter (if available)
   - Returns list of items needing replies
3. Create src/drafts/generator.ts:
   - generateDraft(originalMessage: string, userContext: string, searchMemory: Function): Promise<string>
   - Loads SOUL.md for voice/personality context
   - Searches memory for similar past conversations (RAG) to match user's writing style
   - Calls Anthropic API to generate a draft reply
   - Returns the draft text
4. Create src/drafts/cleaner.ts:
   - cleanExpiredDrafts(workspace: string, ttlHours: number): Promise<number> — moves drafts older than TTL from active/ to expired/, returns count
   - Called during heartbeat
5. Add MCP tools in assistant-server.ts or new drafts-server:
   - draft_list: list active/sent/expired drafts
   - draft_read: read a specific draft
   - draft_approve: mark draft as sent (move to sent/)
   - draft_discard: delete a draft
6. Add workspace/drafts/ directories to memory indexing in collect-files.ts so drafts are searchable

Verification/Test steps:
1. GWT: Given a new draft, When createDraft is called, Then a .md file with YAML frontmatter is created in drafts/active/
2. GWT: Given an active draft, When approveDraft is called, Then draft moves to drafts/sent/
3. GWT: Given active drafts older than TTL, When cleanExpiredDrafts runs, Then they move to drafts/expired/
4. GWT: Given draft_list tool is called, When there are 3 active drafts, Then all 3 are returned with metadata
5. GWT: Given an original email message, When generateDraft is called with SOUL.md context, Then a contextually appropriate reply is generated
6. GWT: Given config.drafts.enabled is false, When heartbeat fires, Then no draft scanning occurs
7. Unit test draft file format (YAML frontmatter parsing)
8. Unit test cleaner with time-mocked drafts
9. Unit test generator with mocked API and memory search
